---
title: "TidyTuesday - week 24"
author: "Maja Urankar"
date: "2026-06-14"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(tidyverse)
```

```{r data}
data_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2026/2026-06-16/"

england_wales_names <- readr::read_csv(
  paste0(data_url, "england_wales_names.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)
ni_names <- readr::read_csv(
  paste0(data_url, "ni_names.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)
scotland_names <- readr::read_csv(
  paste0(data_url, "scotland_names.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)
```

```{r prepare-data}
# England and Wales end in 2024, so 2025 would only represent part of the UK.
data_uk_names <- bind_rows(
  england_wales_names,
  ni_names,
  scotland_names
) %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  filter(between(year, 1997, 2024)) %>%
  group_by(year, sex, name) %>%
  summarise(number = sum(number, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
```

```{r highlight-names}
top_1997_names <- data_uk_names %>%
  filter(year == 1997) %>%
  slice_max(number, n = 5, by = sex, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  arrange(sex, desc(number)) %>%
  select(sex, name) %>%
  mutate(highlight_name = name)

highlight_names <- unique(top_1997_names$name)

name_colors <- setNames(
  c(
    "#176B87", "#2A9D8F", "#C75B12", "#7A5C99", "#B5475A",
    "#A33D73", "#596E9B", "#7A842E", "#B4564D", "#287F78"
  )[seq_along(highlight_names)],
  highlight_names
)
```

```{r plot, fig.width=11, fig.height=7}
plot_data <- data_uk_names %>%
  group_by(year, sex) %>%
  mutate(total_births = sum(number)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  inner_join(top_1997_names, by = c("sex", "name")) %>%
  mutate(share = number / total_births)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(year, share, fill = name)) +
  geom_area(color = "white", linewidth = 0.15) +
  geom_text(
    data = ~ filter(.x, year == 1999),
    aes(label = name),
    position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
    color = "white",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ sex,
    labeller = as_labeller(c(Boy = "Boys", Girl = "Girls"))
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = name_colors,
    breaks = highlight_names
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(2000, 2020, by = 5),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1),
    breaks = seq(0, 0.15, by = 0.025),
    limits = c(0, 0.15),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "The favourite baby names of 1997 are losing their grip",
    subtitle = "Share of published UK baby-name records given one of the five most common names of 1997, by sex",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of published records",
    fill = NULL,
    caption = "Data: ONS, NISRA & National Records of Scotland | TidyTuesday 2026 week 24"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, color = "grey25"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "grey40"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(color = "grey35", margin = margin(r = 8)),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 22, color = "grey20", margin = margin(b = 6)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13, color = "grey35", margin = margin(b = 16)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 8.5, color = "grey45", hjust = 1, margin = margin(t = 12)),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#FAFAF7", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#FAFAF7", color = NA),
    plot.margin = margin(20, 25, 18, 20)
  )

ggsave(
  "tt_2026_w24_uk_baby_names_1997_share.png",
  width = 11,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 400,
  bg = "#FAFAF7"
)
```

```{r name-diversity, fig.width=11, fig.height=7}
different_names <- data_uk_names %>%
  group_by(year, sex) %>%
  summarise(different_names = n_distinct(name), .groups = "drop")

latest_counts <- different_names %>%
  filter(year == max(year)) %>%
  mutate(label = paste0(sex, "  ", scales::comma(different_names)))

ggplot(different_names, aes(year, different_names, color = sex)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.35, lineend = "round") +
  geom_point(
    data = latest_counts,
    size = 3.2
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = latest_counts,
    aes(label = label),
    nudge_x = 0.8,
    hjust = 0,
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 4.2
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(Boy = "#0072B2", Girl = "#CC79A7")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(2000, 2020, by = 5),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.13))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(4000, 9000, by = 1000),
    labels = scales::label_comma(),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.04, 0.08))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "The UK’s baby-name vocabulary is expanding",
    subtitle = "Number of distinct names recorded each year, by sex",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Different names",
    caption = "Data: ONS, NISRA & National Records of Scotland | Names with 2 or fewer births are not published"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey88", linewidth = 0.4),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "grey40"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(color = "grey35", margin = margin(r = 8)),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 22, color = "grey20", margin = margin(b = 6)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13, color = "grey35", margin = margin(b = 16)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 8.5, color = "grey45", hjust = 1, margin = margin(t = 12)),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#FAFAF7", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#FAFAF7", color = NA),
    plot.margin = margin(20, 80, 18, 20)
  )

ggsave(
  "tt_2026_w24_uk_baby_names_different_names.png",
  width = 11,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 400,
  bg = "#FAFAF7"
)
```
